comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PLAN-REVIEW RESULTS (epic + 6 children). 5 PASS (S2-S6), 2 INDETERMINATE (epic, S1). The two INDETERMINATE are NOT content blocks: the deterministic floor (P1-P9) PASSED for both; the LLM semantic pass could not run because the review was executed against the .venv build on branch feat/plan-review-transition-gate while that branch was MID-REFACTOR of the plan-review engine itself (errors: missing schemas/plan_review_verify_inputs_input.schema.json [now present] and prompt undefined-variable ['plan']). A re-run against a settled build should yield real verdicts. The epic's lone advisory (siblings touch same files with no ordering edge) is REMEDIATED: added blocks edges S1->S2, S1->S3, S3->S4, S3->S5, S4->S6, S5->S6. Cross-cutting advisory across S2/S3/S4: capture the snapshot/cache/signing architecture in an ADR (incl. the rejected PID-lease).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: ADVISORY REMEDIATION (re-run round). Folded all valid findings: (CRITICAL, code-grounded) the 'sign over verified_at_sha' design contradicted signing.py — _canonical_payload signs only {v,algorithm,ticket_id,manifest} and head_sha is audit-only, so a new payload field would bump PAYLOAD_VERSION and INVALIDATE every prior signature. Remediated in S4: bind the SHA via the EXISTING signed MANIFEST CHANNEL (a 'verified-at-sha:<sha>' manifest step) — cryptographically bound + reproducible, zero payload/version change, prior signatures still verify; head_sha kept as audit metadata. Also folded: enumerate child IDs + de-risk ordering (S1 primitive first); source=local as documented back-out; ADR for the novel architecture; configurable tunables; per-ticket coverage/test ACs (cross-process flock test, periodic-reverify coverage, CONCURRENT byte-total TOCTOU test, value metric, ENOENT/unsigned test paths, pinned CLI<->MCP names, --source default, error-recovery targets, SHA-resolution fail-closed, head_sha disposition, claim-gate back-out). NOT auto-actioned: the generic deterministic 'AC-quality' heuristic (P6, non-specific); and the DECOMPOSITION concern (reviewer flags S2 [now 18 ACs] and S3 as epic-scale) — surfaced to user as a structural split decision, not silently split. Also: REBAR_LLM_MAX_TOKENS default doubled 8000->16000 (was the cause of S1's truncation; NOT overridden in rebar.toml).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[PASS 1 — T1 Prior-art / novel-architecture justification — review of epic 1f19 + children]

VERDICT: CLEAN for T1. No findings. The bright lines that trigger this overlay (security/auth — HMAC-signed verdicts; novel architectural patterns — content-addressed snapshot layout, delete-on-last-close reader safety, flock GC interlock; deep external integration — git plumbing) are all met with explicit prior-art grounding and justified novelty. Affirmations below.

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria this chunk satisfies):

- [T1(a) prior art considered] STRONG. The epic Context explicitly grounds the design against a named prior-art set — "Gitaly, Sourcegraph gitserver/zoekt, GitHub tarball API, Bazel, ccache, Nix, in-toto" — covering the four relevant lineages: server-side git materialization (Gitaly, gitserver), content-addressed build/artifact caching (Bazel, ccache, Nix), faithful tree export (GitHub tarball API), and signed-attestation envelopes (in-toto). This is not a token name-drop: each maps to a concrete design decision downstream (e.g. content-addressed `<cache>/<sha>/` ← Bazel/ccache/Nix; in-toto Statement shape ← in-toto). location: epic 1f19 "Context" final sentence; S1 6d56 "What"; S4 30b3 "What".

- [T1(a) anti-reinvention] S1 (6d56) does NOT reinvent tree extraction: it explicitly rejects `git archive` with a grounded, accurate enumeration of its lossiness (drops `export-ignore` files, omits submodules, returns LFS pointer files, rewrites `export-subst`) and adopts the established `git read-tree` (into a `GIT_INDEX_FILE` temp index) + `git checkout-index --all --prefix=` idiom instead. That is the correct, well-trodden git-plumbing approach for a faithful committed-tree snapshot — properly chosen over the lossy convenience path, with the rationale stated. location: S1 6d56 "Why" + "What" (faithful tree materialization bullet).

- [T1(b) novelty justified vs established approach] The plan does not hand-wave its novel cross-cutting patterns: the epic's "Architecture decision record" section enumerates them (content-addressed snapshot layout, delete-on-last-close reader safety, flock GC interlock, read-tree+checkout-index over git archive, manifest-channel SHA binding) AND names the REJECTED alternatives (PID+heartbeat lease; payload-schema SHA binding) to be captured in an ADR "so future maintainers see the decision + rejected alternatives." Parent AC8 makes the ADR a closure gate. This is exactly the anti-repackaging discipline T1 asks for. location: epic 1f19 "Architecture decision record" + AC "An ADR captures the architecture + rejected alternatives (PID-lease, payload-schema SHA binding)"; S2 70c7 / S4 30b3 ADR-authoring ACs.

- [T1(b) Rule-of-Three / YAGNI applied] S4b (e308) explicitly runs the Rule-of-Three/YAGNI check before introducing the shared ref-resolution boundary: "there are exactly two consumers today (plan-review hashing + claim-gate re-check); the consolidation is justified by the divergence risk above and is de-risked by an explicit back-out." Consolidating at two consumers is defensible here because the justification is a correctness risk (whole-HEAD vs pinned-SHA divergence re-introducing the staleness false-positive), not speculative generality — and it is paired with a stated reversible back-out. Exemplary handling. location: S4b e308 "What" (Rule-of-Three/YAGNI bullet) + "Back-out" bullet.

- [T1(c) capability assertions resolved] The one load-bearing external-capability assertion — that fetching an arbitrary SHA requires the remote's `uploadpack.allowReachableSHA1InWant` (a server-side config the plan cannot guarantee on, e.g., GitHub) — is RESOLVED at the plan level: S1 documents the prerequisite AND supplies a fallback ("or fetch a containing ref then resolve") plus a `--filter=blob:none` preference over deep shallow refetch. The HMAC-now / DSSE-asymmetric-transparency-log-later posture is likewise framed as a deliberate, documented limitation (S4 shapes the manifest pin as an in-toto Statement so the envelope swap is not a rewrite; limits documented in S6), not an unverified claim. location: S1 6d56 "What" (Fetch prerequisites bullet); S4 30b3 "What" (in-toto envelope-swap bullet).

Net: this plan is, if anything, a model for the T1 overlay — prior art is named and load-bearing, every novel pattern is justified against a rejected alternative and slated for an ADR, the one consolidation passes an explicit Rule-of-Three gate, and the external git-capability dependency is acknowledged with a fallback. No T1 finding.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[PASS 1 review — T11 Data-migration / backfill safety]

VERDICT: T11 is LARGELY NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS. This plan's only persisted-format change is ADDITIVE and forward-only (a new `verified-at-sha:<sha>` manifest step + a queryable `verified_at_sha` ticket field), with no destructive DDL, no blocking lock, and no backfill of historical data. The content-addressed snapshot cache is regenerable/ephemeral (self-healing, reclaimable) — not authoritative persisted data — so it is not a T11 migration. One low-stakes coaching item (an ABSENCE: make the "no historical backfill" decision explicit) is offered below; everything else affirms.

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria this plan satisfies):
- EXPAND-CONTRACT / ONLINE (T11-a): The persisted-format change is pure EXPAND with no contract phase and no blocking DDL. Parent AC2 + S4 (ill-hazel-probe) design the SHA binding as a MANIFEST STEP, NOT a payload-schema change — "no PAYLOAD_VERSION bump, no invalidation of prior signatures" and "prior signatures still verify" (S4 AC1, regression test that pre-existing signatures still verify). `head_sha` audit field is "preserved (additive)". This is the safe expand-only end of the rubric. PASS.
- BACKFILL / SCALE (T11-b): No backfill exists. `verified_at_sha` is set forward-only on new attested runs (set-once-per-SHA); pre-existing closures simply lack the new manifest step and still verify. There is no giant transaction or batched rewrite of historical tickets. PASS by absence-of-backfill.
- RESUMABILITY / IDEMPOTENCY (T11-c): S4 AC3 makes the pin + field write idempotent and serialized via the ticket's existing event-append / optimistic-concurrency boundary (compare-and-set), "set-once per SHA; re-verifying a DIFFERENT SHA overwrites (no stale/double pin)" — re-runnable without double-applying. The snapshot-cache byte-total is reconciled by a startup full-walk (S2 janitor), making cache state self-correcting. PASS.
- DUAL-WRITE WINDOW (T11-d): No backfill→cutover window exists (no backfill), so there are no rows-written-during-migration to lose. Concurrent writes to the new field are handled by the compare-and-set serialization (S4 AC3, proven by a two-writers-racing concurrency test). PASS.
- ROLLBACK / DATA-LOSS (T11-e): Back-out is intrinsic — because the change is additive with no payload-version bump, rolling back the feature leaves prior signatures valid and the extra manifest step/field simply ignorable; `source=local` is the documented behavioral back-out (parent AC3). No destructive transform of existing data means no data-loss path on partial failure. S4 also retires the pin on reopen/abandon so a stale SHA never outlives its closure. PASS.

FINDING (one — ABSENCE, low stakes; raised because the safe decision is implied but never asserted):
- [ ] State explicitly in the plan (parent What/AC2 or S4 ill-hazel-probe) that EXISTING/HISTORICAL certified closures are NOT backfilled with `verified_at_sha` and require NO migration — they remain valid as-is and simply lack the new manifest step.
  - criteria: T11 (a, b)
  - location: parent plan "What" (attested bullet) + Acceptance Criteria line 2 ("no payload-schema/version change; prior signatures still verify"); child S4 `ill-hazel-probe` (30b3) AC1/AC2.
  - evidence: The plan strongly IMPLIES forward-only/no-backfill — "no invalidation of prior signatures", "prior signatures still verify", `verified_at_sha` recorded on attested runs going forward — but never states as a deliberate decision that historical tickets are left untouched and need no data migration. A reviewer/implementer could otherwise read "make verdicts reproducible + bound to verified_at_sha" as motivating a sweep to populate the new field on already-closed tickets, which would re-introduce migration/backfill risk the additive design exists to avoid. Making the no-backfill stance explicit closes that ambiguity and confirms the T11-safe posture by construction.
  - suggested_fix: Add a one-line note to S4 scope/AC and the parent's Out-of-scope: "No backfill of historical closures: pre-existing signed verdicts are not rewritten and carry no `verified_at_sha` manifest step; they remain valid as-is. The field is populated forward-only on new attested runs."

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[PASS 1 — G6 Approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection]

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria this plan satisfies):
- G6 FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: the solution targets the named problem directly — a moving shared checkout producing a false-negative verdict — by reading a snapshot pinned to an immutable SHA rather than a proxy. Not a proxy fix.
- G6 MECHANISM CORRECTNESS (signing channel): the "bind verified_at_sha via the EXISTING manifest channel, not a payload field" decision is verified-correct against the code: signing.py:188-201 `_canonical_payload` signs `{v, algorithm, ticket_id, manifest}` and signs the full `manifest: list[str]` (compute_signature, signing.py:204-206); a `verified-at-sha:<sha>` manifest step therefore enters the signed bytes WITHOUT touching `_canonical_payload` or bumping `PAYLOAD_VERSION`, so prior signatures still verify exactly as the plan claims. Mechanism sound.
- G6 MECHANISM CORRECTNESS (concurrency / TOCTOU): the content-addressed (immutable-SHA-keyed) cache + single-flight + POSIX delete-on-last-close reader model avoids the classic check-then-act race, and the pin write is serialized through the existing optimistic-concurrency/compare-and-set boundary (S4 30b3) — the atomicity mechanism is NAMED, not merely asserted. read-tree+checkout-index over `git archive` is correctly justified (archive drops export-ignore/LFS/export-subst). Edge/concurrent/failure cases (fetch-auth fail-closed, ENOENT→re-materialize, crashed-materialization tmp/ sweep) are handled.
- G6 APPROACH SELECTION: I generated and weighed structurally-different alternatives — (a) per-ref `git worktree` checkouts instead of content-addressed materialized snapshots, (b) `git archive` tarball materialization, (c) a new signed-payload SHA field instead of the manifest channel. Each is inferior on the dimensions that matter here (worktrees take index/HEAD locks and contend under concurrency; archive is lossy/unfaithful for an attestation; a payload field invalidates every prior signature). The chosen approach is defensibly at-least-as-good on codebase-alignment, blast-radius, and robustness. No clearly-superior missed alternative — PASS, alternatives discarded.
- G6 POSITIVE RATIONALE: present and explicit (de-risking order "riskiest piece first"; why manifest-channel over payload-schema; why read-tree over archive; design grounded against Gitaly/Sourcegraph/Bazel/Nix/in-toto). PASS.

FINDING (G6 — MECHANISM CORRECTNESS: the chosen approach's data-flow is incomplete for the plan-review gate; its verdict-producing hash path still reads the mutable checkout, so the plan does not fully achieve its own AC1/AC2 for that gate as decomposed):
- criteria: G6 (mechanism actually achieves the goal — data-flow complete, no path left reading the wrong source).
- location: parent AC1 ("No code-reading gate reads the server's working tree in attested mode") + AC2 (reproducible); "What" bullet "point the gate's file tools (pai_tools root / cfg.repo_path) at the snapshot dir" (S3 fa49 What/Scope/file_impact); S4b e308 file_impact; src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py:106, :195, :325.
- evidence:
  1. The plan re-roots only the LLM *file tools* onto the snapshot (S3 fa49: "point the gate's file tools (pai_tools root / cfg.repo_path) at the snapshot dir"; file_impact = completion.py / pai_tools.py / mcp_server.py).
  2. But the plan-review gate's verdict/attestation hashing is computed elsewhere, all rooted on the MUTABLE checkout via `_config.repo_root`: `dependency_hashes()` (attest.py:106-107) signs the file_impact map from the working tree; `_rehash()` (attest.py:191-196, "Re-hash … at CURRENT working-tree content"); the ADR-0002 claim-gate freshness re-check (attest.py:325-326). None reads a snapshot dir.
  3. The plan DID create a dedicated child for this coherence — S4b `piney-gold-day` (e308) — but its file_impact is `src/rebar/signing.py` + the ADR doc, NOT `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py` where the three hash sites actually live. So no child re-roots attest.py onto the pinned-SHA snapshot.
  4. Consequence (mechanism gap, not just ownership): an attested plan-review signs file_impact hashes, but the later claim-gate freshness re-check re-hashes the same paths from the server working tree (possibly a different branch / dirty) → spurious "stale-code" drift, OR a re-hash that silently passes against the WRONG branch — re-introducing exactly the moving-checkout false-verdict this epic exists to eliminate. The plan's stated mechanism ("never the server's mutable checkout") is therefore not achieved for the plan-review gate's hashing path.
- checklist_item: - [ ] Close the plan-review hashing data-flow: make `attest.py`'s `dependency_hashes()` (:106), `_rehash()` (:195), and the ADR-0002 freshness re-check (:325) hash from the pinned-SHA snapshot basis rather than `_config.repo_root`, so the plan-review gate's verdict/attestation path actually reads the snapshot (AC1/AC2) — and name `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py` in the file_impact of whichever child owns it (S3 fa49 or S4b e308), since today no child lists it.
- suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py` to S4b (e308) file_impact and extend its shared-ref-resolution boundary to route the pinned-SHA snapshot base into all three hash sites (dependency_hashes / _rehash / freshness re-check) in place of `_config.repo_root`; alternatively fold those three sites into S3's re-rooting scope. Either way, exactly one child must name attest.py so the snapshot basis reaches the hashing path the signed verdict depends on.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[PASS 1 — T1 Prior-art / novel-architecture justification] Verdict: PASS (overlay fired; well-grounded). One minor coaching item.

This plan crosses several T1 bright-lines (novel cross-cutting architecture, security/auth signing, a content-addressed cache, portability/concurrency target) so the overlay applies — and the plan handles it unusually well.

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria satisfied — positive feedback, not findings):
- T1(a) prior art is considered before committing: the parent "Context"/"What" explicitly grounds the design against Gitaly, Sourcegraph gitserver/zoekt, GitHub tarball API, Bazel, ccache, Nix, and in-toto; the janitor child (b73c) cites Bazel's move-GC-to-idle and the kernel-guarantee posture of Gitaly/Sourcegraph/Bazel/ccache as the basis for rejecting a PID+heartbeat lease. This is real, named, load-bearing prior art — not reinvention.
- T1(b) novelty justified vs an established approach: the rejected alternatives are explicit and captured in an ADR (PID+heartbeat lease rejected from a spike as "unsound: N readers, PID reuse, crash-stale"; payload-schema SHA binding rejected in favor of the manifest channel; `git archive` rejected for read-tree+checkout-index on concrete faithfulness grounds — export-ignore drops, submodule omission, LFS pointer files, export-subst rewrite). S4b (e308) carries an explicit Rule-of-Three / YAGNI check ("exactly two consumers today ... justified by the divergence risk ... de-risked by an explicit back-out") for the shared ref-resolution boundary. Anti-repackaging discipline is satisfied.
- T1(c) capability assertions largely resolved: `git archive` lossiness, `uploadpack.allowReachableSHA1InWant` (with a "fetch a containing ref then resolve" fallback), `--filter=blob:none`, POSIX delete-on-last-close, the relatime/noatime atime caveat (use mtime touch-on-read instead), and the EFS/NFS flock caveat (tracked on alto-fruit-punch, with an atomic-mkdir fallback) are all stated with fallbacks/limits rather than left as bare "library supports X" assertions.

FINDING (minor — capability assertion partially unresolved):
- [ ] Validate the "in-toto Statement → DSSE/asymmetric envelope swap" forward-path claim with a concrete shape, not just a contract test. (criteria: T1(c) unverified capability assertions resolved. location: S4 `ill-hazel-probe` (30b3) "What" — "Shape the manifest pin so a future move to DSSE + asymmetric + transparency log is an envelope swap, not a rewrite (KEEP HMAC now)" + AC6; parent "What" attested bullet. evidence: the claim that a later DSSE/asymmetric/transparency-log migration is "an envelope swap, not a rewrite" is the one prior-art-derived capability assertion that is asserted rather than demonstrated — the pin currently rides the HMAC manifest-step channel (`verified-at-sha:<sha>` as a manifest list element), whereas an in-toto Statement is a structured JSON predicate/subject; whether a manifest-step string can be re-homed into a DSSE Statement "without changing the pin's data shape" is load-bearing for the no-rewrite claim and is not shown. AC6 asks for "a test/contract" on the data shape, which is good, but does not pin down WHAT the in-toto subject/predicate fields are.) suggested_fix: In S4 (30b3), add a one-line concrete sketch of the in-toto Statement subject/predicate the pin maps to (e.g. subject = {name: ticket_id, digest: {sha1: <verified_at_sha>}}, predicateType = the verdict), so the "envelope swap not rewrite" claim is demonstrably grounded in the in-toto v1 schema rather than asserted — and the AC6 contract test asserts against that named shape.

Net: T1 is satisfied. The plan's prior-art grounding and rejected-alternatives discipline are a model for the rubric; the single finding above is a polish item on one forward-compatibility capability claim, not a blocker.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — T8 (LLM / prompt structural-completeness probe) [overlay]: CLEAN — no findings. The plan defines an LLM/agent gate system (five code-reading gates + a `source` enum + signed-manifest verdicts), so the overlay applies; I probed it for the five structural-gap classes and found each affirmatively specified (tool-verified against current source).

Affirmations (criteria this chunk satisfies):
- [T8] No referenced schema/enum left with an undefined value vocabulary. The `source` enum is fully enumerated `{attested|local}` (parent What; S3 fa49; S5 b7fa), and `ref` is `{branch|tag|SHA}` default `origin/main`. S5 b7fa AC explicitly handles an INVALID `--source` value with an actionable error naming the allowed enum — i.e. the vocabulary is both defined and validated at the surface.
- [T8] The processing/decision rule is co-located with the schema that needs it. The load-bearing rule "attested signs / local never signs" is decomposed as a clean producer/enforcer split that each ticket states locally and cross-references: S3 fa49 marks the run unsigned (AC4), S4 30b3 bars signing (AC3) and is the single place the `verified-at-sha:<sha>` manifest step is written, S5 b7fa reflects unsigned in the result (AC2). No orphaned rule.
- [T8] No counter/state increment with ambiguous placement. The `verified_at_sha` state write has an explicitly named placement + serialization: set-once-per-SHA, overwrite-on-reverify, retire-on-reopen/abandon, serialized through the ticket's event-append / optimistic-concurrency compare-and-set boundary (S4 30b3 What + AC2/AC3, "NOT verified by inspection").
- [T8] Fallbacks for incomplete/failed sub-steps are specified across the chain: unresolvable ref in attested mode → fail closed, no signature (S4 30b3 AC; S1 6d56 AC5); fetch/auth failure → descriptive actionable credential error, attested fails closed / local still runs (S1 6d56 AC5); absent/being-GC'd snapshot dir → ENOENT → re-materialize, never read a partial tree (S3 fa49 AC6); invalid `--source` / unreachable object DB at REBAR_ROOT → clear actionable error, tested (S5 b7fa AC4).
- [T8] No instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern; referenced agents/skills/enums exist and are fully specified (tool-verified). All five named gates resolve to real operations — `review_plan` (mcp_server.py:656, plan_review/__init__.py:50), `verify_completion` (mcp_server.py:635, completion.py:169), `review_code` (mcp_server.py:595, code_review.py:111), `scan_spec` (mcp_server.py:618), and `review_ticket`. The "EXISTING SIGNED MANIFEST CHANNEL" is a real shared primitive (`signing.sign_manifest` signing.py:321; `build_manifest`/`manifest_deps`/`manifest_regver` in plan_review/attest.py), and the proposed `verified-at-sha:<sha>` manifest step is structurally consistent with that existing manifest-line family (parsed analogously to `dep <sha> <path>` / `regver`), so it needs no `_canonical_payload`/`PAYLOAD_VERSION` change — the plan's reproducibility/no-prior-signature-breakage claim is well-founded.

Scope note (NOT a T8 finding): the standing S3↔S4 seam — plan_review/attest.py's `dependency_hashes()` (attest.py:106-107), `_rehash()` (:191-196), and the ADR-0002 freshness re-check (:325-326) still hashing from `_config.repo_root` (the mutable checkout) rather than the pinned-SHA snapshot — is a real cross-child consistency/dependency-routing concern, but it is a G4 matter (WHO threads the snapshot handle), not an LLM-structural gap: no enum, decision rule, counter, or fallback is left undefined by it. It is already filed on S4 (30b3) and is out of T8's scope.

Conclusion: clean chunk for T8 — empty findings list; the LLM/agent gate system's enums, decision rules, state-write placement, fallbacks, and referenced operations are all fully specified and tool-verified.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[PASS 1 review — G6 Approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection]

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 criteria this plan satisfies):
- Mechanism correctness (core path): the resolve→pin-immutable-SHA→faithful-materialize (read-tree into a temp GIT_INDEX_FILE + checkout-index, NOT git archive)→content-addressed cache→run gate→bind SHA flow is logically complete and concurrency-aware. Single-flight, POSIX delete-on-last-close reader safety, atomic dir-rename population, and the flock GC interlock handle the concurrent/eviction/crash edge cases without a check-then-act race. The attested pin write names a real anti-TOCTOU serialization (compare-and-set on the ticket's existing event-append/optimistic-concurrency boundary), not just an assertion.
- Manifest-channel SHA binding is mechanism-correct and verified against source: `signing._canonical_payload` signs `{v, algorithm, ticket_id, manifest}` and `compute_signature` signs the full `manifest: list[str]` (signing.py:188-206), so a `verified-at-sha:<sha>` MANIFEST STEP enters the signed bytes WITHOUT a `_canonical_payload`/`PAYLOAD_VERSION` change — the "no prior-signature invalidation" claim holds. This is a genuinely good design choice (additive, reproducible) over the rejected new-signed-payload-field approach.
- Fitness-for-purpose: the wrong-branch false-negative that motivated the epic is directly targeted by the AC4 behavioral regression in S3 (attested verdict IDENTICAL regardless of the server's checked-out branch) — a behavioral contract, not a change-detector. local mode preserves the single-dev verify-before-push back-out.
- Approach selection (alternatives generated and judged): (1) `git archive` materialization — the plan explicitly rejects it on faithfulness (export-ignore/LFS/submodule/export-subst), correct. (2) `git worktree add` per ref instead of a content-addressed snapshot dir — would take worktree/index.lock locks and reintroduce exactly the contention the lock-free no-worktree approach avoids; the chosen approach is defensibly superior on concurrency + blast-radius. Both alternatives discarded; the chosen approach is defensible.
- Positive rationale is present and strong (immutable SHA = perfect cache key / no staleness; manifest channel chosen to avoid signature invalidation; de-risking order; design grounded against Gitaly/Sourcegraph/Bazel/Nix/in-toto). G6 rationale criterion PASSES — no finding there.

FINDING (mechanism / fitness-for-purpose — the named re-rooting approach under-specifies the plan-review attestation hashing path, so AC1/AC2 "never the mutable checkout" may not actually hold for the claim gate):

- [ ] State HOW the pinned-SHA snapshot basis reaches plan-review's `dependency_hashes()` / `_rehash()` / the ADR-0002 claim-gate freshness re-check, since the plan's chosen mechanism ("re-root the gate's file tools / pai_tools root / cfg.repo_path") does not cover those three sign/verify-side hashing sites — all of which root on the MUTABLE checkout via `_config.repo_root` (attest.py:106-107, :191-196, :325-326). As decomposed, AC2's reproducibility and AC1's "no code-reading gate reads the server's working tree in attested mode" are not achieved for the plan-review claim gate: a signed verdict can hash file_impact from the snapshot while the later freshness re-check re-hashes the same paths from whatever branch/dirty tree the server is on — reintroducing the very moving-checkout false-verdict the epic exists to kill (now on the verify side).
  - criteria: G6 (mechanism correctness — a hidden read-root divergence that defeats the goal; fitness-for-purpose — solves the named problem for the LLM file-tool gates but not the claim-gate hashing path).
  - location: parent "What" (attested bullet: "run gate"), Success Criteria ("every code-reading gate ... never the server's mutable checkout"), Acceptance Criteria AC1 + AC2; mechanism owned across S3 fa49 ("point the gate's file tools (pai_tools root / cfg.repo_path) at the snapshot dir") and S4b dodgy-luff-quasar (claim-gate↔plan-review ref coherence).
  - evidence:
    1. `dependency_hashes()` signs the dep map hashed from `base = str(_config.repo_root(repo_root))` (attest.py:106-107) — the mutable checkout, not a snapshot dir.
    2. `_rehash()` re-hashes "at CURRENT working-tree content" from `_config.repo_root` (attest.py:191-196).
    3. The ADR-0002 freshness re-check hashes from `_config.repo_root` (attest.py:325-326).
    4. The plan describes the gate-read mechanism only as re-rooting the "file tools (pai_tools root / cfg.repo_path)"; attest.py's hashing path is a separate code root not covered by that phrasing, and no part of the parent text names it as in-scope for the snapshot basis. (This is the same seam the standing S4 30b3 G4 dependency-gap notes; raising it here at G6 because, at the parent level, it is an approach/mechanism completeness gap — the chosen "re-root file tools" mechanism is not sufficient to satisfy the plan's own AC1/AC2 for the claim gate.)
  - suggested_fix: In the parent "What"/Acceptance, make the snapshot read-root explicit for the plan-review attestation hashing path (not just the LLM file tools): thread the resolved-SHA snapshot dir as the hash `base` into `dependency_hashes()`, `_rehash()`, and the ADR-0002 freshness re-check in place of `_config.repo_root`, and assign that re-rooting unambiguously to one child (extend S3's re-rooting scope to include plan_review/attest.py, or claim it explicitly in S4b) so AC1/AC2 are mechanically achievable for the claim gate.

No other G6 findings: the core snapshot/cache/signing mechanism is sound, the named problem is solved, the chosen approach is defensible against the two structurally-different alternatives generated, and positive rationale is present.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[PASS 1 review — T1 Prior-art / novel-architecture justification (overlay; bright-lines: novel architectural pattern + security/signing)]

AFFIRMATIONS (T1 criteria this plan satisfies):
- Prior-art considered (T1a) — PASS. The parent Context explicitly grounds the design against a broad, relevant prior-art set: "Gitaly, Sourcegraph gitserver/zoekt, GitHub tarball API, Bazel, ccache, Nix, in-toto." These are the right reference systems for a content-addressed, concurrency-safe repo-snapshot+attestation primitive (git-server materialization, content-addressed build caches, supply-chain attestation). Not reinventing in a vacuum.
- Novelty justified vs established approach (T1b) — PASS. The two genuinely novel choices are each justified against a named established alternative the design REJECTS with a concrete reason: (i) "read-tree+checkout-index over `git archive`" is justified by enumerated git-archive faithfulness defects (S1 6d56 Why: drops export-ignore files, omits submodules, returns LFS pointer files, rewrites export-subst) — anti-repackaging is satisfied because the off-the-shelf option is shown insufficient for the attestation guarantee; (ii) "dropping the rejected PID+heartbeat lease" in favour of POSIX delete-on-last-close is a documented spike OUTCOME (S2 70c7 triage note item 5; ADR AC). The cross-cutting ADR (authored in S2/S4) capturing "the decision + rejected alternatives" is exactly the Rule-of-Three / anti-repackaging artifact T1 asks for, and parent AC8 makes the ADR a closeable obligation.
- Capability assertion — signing channel (T1c) — PASS/RESOLVED. The "bind the SHA via the existing manifest channel without a PAYLOAD_VERSION bump / prior signatures still verify" claim is the one security-sensitive capability assertion, and it is RESOLVED against current source (signing.py: _canonical_payload signs {v,algorithm,ticket_id,manifest}; compute_signature signs the full manifest list; PAYLOAD_VERSION untouched by a manifest step) — already verified on S4 30b3. Not an unverified "library supports X."

FINDING:
- [ ] Resolve the unverified capability assertion that the chosen `git read-tree`+`checkout-index` primitive yields a FAITHFUL tree for LFS-tracked paths and submodules — name the mechanism, since the primitive does not provide it natively. (criteria: T1c unverified-capability-assertion) — location: S1 `hoot-glove-foyer` (6d56) "What" (FAITHFUL tree materialization bullet) + AC2 ("LFS pointer files are detected (not silently served as content); submodule handling is defined and documented"); parent "What" (attested faithful materialization). evidence: The plan asserts the OUTCOME ("LFS pointer files are detected", "submodule handling is defined") but the chosen primitive does not deliver it on its own — `git checkout-index` materializes the committed blob content, which for an LFS-tracked path IS the ~130-byte `version https://git-lfs…` pointer (not real content) and for a submodule is a gitlink (mode 160000) that checkout-index does not populate. So the very faithfulness guarantee that justifies the read-tree+checkout-index novelty over `git archive` (S1 Why explicitly lists git-archive's "returns LFS pointer files / omits submodules" as the defect it fixes) is, for these two cases, NOT yet shown to be delivered by the replacement — the capability is asserted, not resolved to a named command/decision. This is the load-bearing T1(b)/(c) gap: the anti-`git archive` justification only holds if the replacement actually closes the LFS/submodule faithfulness hole it claims as its motivation. suggested_fix: Add one sentence to S1's "What" pinning the mechanism: for LFS, name the detection path (e.g. `.gitattributes` `filter=lfs` and/or the pointer-file magic header) and STATE the decision — fetch real LFS content vs. surface the pointer as a documented known-limitation (and reflect that limitation in S6 docs); for submodules, name the enumeration (e.g. `git ls-tree` gitlink detection) and the recurse-vs-omit decision. If either resolution is non-trivial, carve a short SPIKE so the faithfulness claim that anchors the over-`git-archive` novelty is verified rather than assumed.

NOTE (not a T1 finding): the parent Context cites the "GitHub tarball API" as a design influence, but "Sparse archiving" / any HTTPS tarball fetch is explicitly Out of scope and no child calls that endpoint — so it is a considered-and-rejected prior-art reference, not an unverified external-capability assertion. Recorded so a later pass does not re-raise it.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[PASS 1 review — T3 Integration feasibility (epic-level, parent plan "Repo-snapshot isolation for code-reading gates")]

AFFIRMATIONS (T3 criteria this plan satisfies):
- technical_feasibility / named-subcommand existence — MATCH for the core git plumbing. The materialization primitive's chosen commands (`git fetch origin`, `git rev-parse`/ref→immutable-SHA resolution, `git read-tree <sha>` into a temp index via `GIT_INDEX_FILE`, `git checkout-index --all --prefix=<tmp>/`) are all real git subcommands, and git-as-subprocess is an ALREADY-USED integration in this repo (verified: src/rebar/_commands/{compact,delete,fsck,fsck_recover,txn,init,purge_bridge}.py and src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py all shell `["git", ...]`; signing.py head_sha() uses `git rev-parse`). Extending that established subprocess seam is not a new external-capability class — the base integration is NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS.
- The attested-signing manifest channel (S4) is an INTERNAL already-used integration extended additively (signing._canonical_payload signs {v,algorithm,ticket_id,manifest}; the manifest step rides existing signed bytes with no PAYLOAD_VERSION bump) — T3 not-applicable / PASS for the signing channel.
- auth/HTTPS preconditions stated — PASS. The plan/children pre-empt the arbitrary-SHA fetch precondition (`uploadpack.allowReachableSHA1InWant` or fetch-a-containing-ref-then-resolve; `--filter=blob:none`) and route private-repo credential failure to a descriptive, actionable error (S1 AC5, parent AC5).
- SPIKE posture — PASS. The de-risking order front-loads the lock-free `read-tree`+`checkout-index` primitive as a thin vertical slice that proves the capability before the horizontal sweep, which functions as the spike T3 asks for. The rejected alternative (`git archive`) is named.
- portability primitives — PASS. `fcntl.flock` + atomic-`mkdir` fallback and `tempfile.gettempdir()`/`mkdtemp` are stdlib; no external `tar` (parent AC6).

FINDING:

- [ ] Name the concrete git mechanism by which `read-tree`+`checkout-index` reproduces (or intentionally diverges from) the `export-ignore` / `export-subst` / submodule / LFS-pointer semantics, or route those sub-capabilities to a SPIKE — the plan asserts a "faithful" snapshot and the children assert "LFS pointer files are detected" + "submodule handling is defined" (S1 6d56 AC2) as OUTCOMES without naming HOW, and `read-tree`/`checkout-index` are not yet used anywhere in this repo (verified: zero matches for `read-tree`/`checkout-index`), so this is the one materialization sub-capability whose feasibility is asserted-but-unverified.
  - criteria: T3 technical_feasibility; T3 named-subcommand existence = UNVERIFIED for the faithfulness sub-clauses.
  - location: parent "What" (attested: "faithful lock-free materialization into a content-addressed snapshot") + parent AC1 ("a faithful snapshot materialized at the pinned SHA"); S1 6d56 "What" (FAITHFUL tree materialization bullet) + S1 6d56 AC1/AC2.
  - evidence:
    1. The EXISTING snapshot path documents archive-NATIVE behaviors that `checkout-index` does NOT replicate: src/rebar/llm/workflow/snapshot.py:23-27 — "`git archive` ... `export-ignore` paths are omitted and `export-subst` is applied (git archive does this natively); **submodule** contents are NOT included (archive stops at the gitlink); **Git-LFS** files appear as their pointer text". `export-ignore`/`export-subst` are git-ARCHIVE attributes; `git read-tree`+`git checkout-index` honor NEITHER and materialize the committed blob content as-is.
    2. For an LFS-tracked path the committed blob content IS the ~130-byte `version https://git-lfs…` pointer; for a submodule the tree entry is a gitlink (mode 160000) that `checkout-index` does not populate. So S1 6d56 AC2's "LFS pointer files are detected (not silently served as content)" and "submodule handling is defined" are precisely the cases the chosen primitive does NOT resolve on its own — the plan asserts the outcome without a named command.
    3. `read-tree`/`checkout-index` are NEW to this repo (search: zero matches), unlike the already-used `git archive`/`rev-parse`/`fetch` seam — so the faithfulness contract rides on a primitive that has not been exercised here.
  - suggested_fix: In the parent "What" (and S1 6d56's "What"/AC2) name the LFS-detection mechanism (e.g. `.gitattributes` `filter=lfs` and/or the LFS pointer-file magic header; document whether real LFS content is fetched or the pointer is surfaced as a known limitation) and the submodule mechanism (e.g. `git ls-tree` gitlink enumeration; document recurse-vs-omit), AND state explicitly whether `read-tree`+`checkout-index` reproduces or intentionally diverges from `git archive`'s `export-ignore`/`export-subst` semantics documented in snapshot.py:23-27 (if it diverges, name the fidelity contract the gates rely on). Alternatively split LFS/submodule/export-attr faithfulness into a short SPIKE child, since it is the one materialization sub-capability not yet pinned to a named, repo-exercised command.

NOTE (not a finding): the GitHub tarball API cited in the parent Context as a design influence is explicitly Out of scope ("Sparse archiving"; the materialize path is git-plumbing-only, no HTTPS tarball fetch). No child calls that API, so there is no asserted-but-unverified external endpoint — T3 not-applicable to the tarball reference; recorded so a later pass does not re-raise it as a missing-auth/endpoint gap.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[PASS 1 — G6 Approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection]

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 criteria this plan satisfies):
- Mechanism correctness (core): the resolve-ref→immutable-SHA → faithful `git read-tree`+`checkout-index` materialization → content-addressed `<cache>/<sha>/` cache → delete-on-last-close reader safety → flock GC interlock chain is logically complete and explicitly handles the concurrent/failure/edge cases (single-flight collapse, ENOENT→re-materialize self-heal, atomic dir-rename all-or-nothing population, crash leaves only tmp/ swept on startup, no-PID-lease liveness). The TOCTOU surface is named and closed where it matters (S2b AC4 byte-total under concurrent populate-vs-evict; S4 AC3 compare-and-set pin serialized through the event-append boundary).
- Fitness-for-purpose (signing): the manifest-channel SHA binding is verified against real source — `signing._canonical_payload` signs `{v, algorithm, ticket_id, manifest}` and `compute_signature` signs the full `manifest: list[str]`, so a `verified-at-sha:<sha>` manifest STEP enters the signed bytes without a `PAYLOAD_VERSION` bump or prior-signature invalidation. The "bind WHAT code was verified" goal is genuinely solved, not proxied.
- Positive rationale present: the plan states WHY for each non-obvious choice — faithful read-tree+checkout-index over lossy `git archive` (export-ignore/export-subst/LFS/submodule fidelity), manifest-channel over payload-schema bump (no signature invalidation), delete-on-last-close over the rejected PID+heartbeat lease — each grounded in named prior art (Gitaly, Sourcegraph gitserver/zoekt, Bazel, ccache, Nix, in-toto). The ADR captures the cross-cutting decisions. This satisfies the G6 positive-rationale requirement.
- Approach selection: I generated two structurally-different alternatives — (a) `git worktree add` per ref, and (b) a bare mirror clone with per-ref `GIT_DIR` object-DB reads — and judge the chosen approach defensibly at-least-as-good: worktrees take repo-level index/config locks (the exact contention parent AC4 sets out to eliminate) and still need a checkout to yield a faithful tree; the content-addressed snapshot is more testable (immutable SHA = perfect cache key, no staleness) and lower blast-radius (object DB untouched, reads off a copied tree). No clearly-superior alternative was missed. PASS — alternatives discarded, not written into the plan.

FINDING (G6 — fitness-for-purpose / hidden data-flow gap: the plan-review claim-gate path still hashes the MUTABLE working tree, so the named goal "never the server's mutable checkout" is unmet for that one gate)
- criteria: G6 (1) mechanism correctness — hidden data-flow incompleteness; G6 (2) solves the named problem, not a proxy.
- location: parent AC1 ("No code-reading gate reads the server's working tree in attested mode") + AC2 (reproducible against the pinned SHA); S4b `piney-gold-day` (e308) AC1/AC2 + file_impact (signing.py); S3 `pale-peer-hawk` (fa49) Scope/file_impact; src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py.
- evidence:
  1. THREE sign/verify-side hashing sites in attest.py all root on the MUTABLE checkout via `_config.repo_root`, not on a snapshot dir: `dependency_hashes()` (attest.py:106-107, docstring line 92 "hashed from the working tree"), `_rehash()` (attest.py:191-196, "Re-hash ... at CURRENT working-tree content"), and the ADR-0002 claim-gate freshness re-check (attest.py:325-326), with the unscoped whole-HEAD fallback at attest.py:338 also resolving through `_config.repo_root`.
  2. S4b (e308) consolidates these into "ONE shared ref-resolution boundary" in signing.py (its AC1, file_impact src/rebar/signing.py) — but its What/AC describe collapsing TWO resolution SITES into one; nothing states that the shared boundary hashes from the PINNED-SHA SNAPSHOT DIR rather than from the working tree. Consolidating two mutable-tree hash sites into one mutable-tree boundary still hashes the mutable tree.
  3. S3 (fa49) re-roots only "the gate's file tools (pai_tools root / cfg.repo_path)"; its file_impact is completion.py/pai_tools.py/mcp_server.py — NOT attest.py. So the snapshot re-rooting that satisfies AC1 for the LLM gates never reaches the plan-review attestation hashing path.
  4. No child's file_impact lists attest.py; the snapshot-handle→hashing-path data-flow is described by no ticket.
- why this is a G6 (not merely G4) concern: independent of WHO owns the seam, the mechanism as decomposed does not achieve the parent's stated goal for the plan-review/claim-gate gate — an attested plan-review would sign `file_impact` hashes and the claim-gate would re-hash, both off whatever branch/dirty state the shared checkout happens to be in. That re-introduces exactly the moving-checkout false-verdict the epic exists to kill (the motivating session bug), so the solution as written is a proxy for the named problem on this path, not a fix.
- checklist_item: - [ ] State, in S4b (e308) and/or S3, that the shared ref-resolution boundary hashes `file_impact`/freshness-re-check paths from the PINNED-SHA SNAPSHOT (the materialized tree), not from `_config.repo_root` — so attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/ADR-0002 re-check read the snapshot, satisfying parent AC1's "never the server's working tree" for the plan-review gate; add attest.py to the owning child's file_impact and a covering AC.
- suggested_fix: In S4b's "What", change "ONE shared ref-resolution boundary" to specify that the boundary resolves the client ref to the pinned SHA AND hashes paths from the snapshot dir materialized at that SHA (the S1 handle), replacing `_config.repo_root` as the hash base in dependency_hashes() (attest.py:106-107), _rehash() (attest.py:191-196), and the freshness re-check (attest.py:325-326); list src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py in S4b's file_impact and add an AC ("the shared boundary hashes from the pinned-SHA snapshot, not the working tree"). The existing S4b negative test (its AC2, "both gates hash at the SAME ref") should additionally assert that ref is the pinned SHA's snapshot content, not the current checkout.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "Plan unchanged; PASS plan-review attestation is stale only because HEAD moved (signed at 786961983, HEAD dca5cf20). Implementing epic per user request.".

compiled_state.description:
# Repo-snapshot isolation for code-reading gates

## Context

The rebar MCP server is a long-lived process pinned to ONE working directory. Every operation
that reads PROJECT SOURCE CODE (the LLM gates) reads whatever branch + uncommitted changes
happen to be in that checkout at call time. This session hit it: a completion-verifier read
the wrong branch (a parallel task had switched the shared checkout) and produced a FALSE-
NEGATIVE on work correctly merged to main. Broader problem: an MCP server used by a DISTRIBUTED
TEAM concurrently cannot have all gates read one mutable working tree, and an HMAC-signed
verdict computed against a moving branch is not reproducible. Design grounded against Gitaly,
Sourcegraph gitserver/zoekt, GitHub tarball API, Bazel, ccache, Nix, in-toto.

## What

Every code-reading op accepts `ref` (branch|tag|SHA, default `origin/main`) + `source` mode:
- **attested** (default): fetch -> resolve ref to immutable SHA -> faithful lock-free
  materialization into a content-addressed snapshot -> run gate -> record `verified_at_sha`
  and bind it via the EXISTING SIGNED MANIFEST CHANNEL (a manifest step, NOT a payload-schema
  change — so no PAYLOAD_VERSION bump, no invalidation of prior signatures; see S4). Reproducible.
- **local** (opt-in): read the server's in-place checkout (possibly dirty); NEVER signs.

## Children (de-risking order)

Riskiest piece first — the lock-free portable snapshot primitive — then build outward:
1. S1 `hoot-glove-foyer` (6d56) — faithful, lock-free materialization core (the primitive).
2. S2 `clay-feast-strife` (70c7) — content-addressed snapshot cache: single-flight + reader-safety (depends on S1).
3. S2b `dodgy-luff-quasar` (b73c) — snapshot cache JANITOR: reclamation under disk pressure (depends on S2).
4. S3 `pale-peer-hawk` (fa49) — thread ref+source through the 5 gates (depends on S1).
5. S4 `ill-hazel-probe` (30b3) — attested signing: pin verified_at_sha via the manifest channel (depends on S3).
6. S4b `piney-gold-day` (e308) — claim-gate<->plan-review ref-resolution coherence + ADR 0002 (depends on S4).
7. S5 `harm-shove-gate` (b7fa) — CLI + MCP surfaces (depends on S3).
8. S6 `vain-weed-rave` (1ba5) — docs (depends on S4, S5).
Blocking edges encode this DAG. A thin vertical slice (S1 + the cache + one gate re-rooted
end-to-end) proves the snapshot primitive before the horizontal sweep.

## Out of scope

Sparse archiving; verdict memoization; auto ticket->commit provenance (REB-289); diff-base
(base+head) for code-review (single ref/snapshot like the others); serverless lifecycle (AWS
volume placement + EFS/NFS flock caveat tracked on alto-fruit-punch).

## Architecture decision record

The novel cross-cutting patterns (content-addressed snapshot layout, delete-on-last-close
reader safety, flock GC interlock, read-tree+checkout-index over `git archive`, dropping the
rejected PID+heartbeat lease, manifest-channel SHA binding) are captured in an ADR (authored
in S2/S4) so future maintainers see the decision + rejected alternatives.

## Success Criteria

Every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot at a
pinned SHA — never the server's mutable checkout — safely under concurrent distributed use;
signed verdicts bind `verified_at_sha` via the manifest channel (reproducible, no prior-
signature breakage); local mode preserves the single-developer "verify before push" workflow.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No code-reading gate reads the server's working tree in attested mode; all read a
      faithful snapshot materialized at the pinned SHA.
- [ ] A signed verdict binds `verified_at_sha` via the signed manifest channel (no payload-
      schema/version change; prior signatures still verify) and is reproducible against that SHA.
- [ ] `local` source mode reads the in-place checkout and CANNOT produce a signed verdict;
      source=local is the documented back-out that restores the prior in-place read behavior.
- [ ] Concurrent gates on the same and different SHAs run without git-lock contention or
      cross-op corruption; the snapshot cache + janitor reclaim disk under a defined free-space
      watermark (S2) without disrupting in-flight reads.
- [ ] Private-repo fetch failures surface a descriptive, actionable credential error.
- [ ] Portable across macOS + Linux (no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback).
- [ ] Operational tunables (free-space watermark, default ref, grace/age windows) are
      configurable with documented defaults (S2/S3).
- [ ] An ADR captures the architecture + rejected alternatives (PID-lease, payload-schema SHA binding).
- [ ] All EIGHT enumerated child stories (above) closed; `make lint` + full test suite green.
compiled_state.title: Repo-snapshot isolation for code-reading gates (client ref, attested signing, concurrency-safe portable snapshots)
